steps.title: Fit the table as an exponential
steps.content: Use the exponential form $y=a\cdot b^{x}$. The table values show the multiplicative pattern, so the growth factor is $b=69$. Substituting one table point gives $a=1$.

steps.title: Determine direction of exponential function
steps.content: The same pattern is clear by ratios: $1\div \frac{1}{69}=69$, $69\div1=69$, and $4761\div69=69$. The constant ratio means exponential, and because $69\gt 1$, the function is increasing. That confirms C.
